full_prompt: Design the primary experiment mounting frame installed inside an ESA BEXUS stratospheric balloon gondola for a university team experiment. The BEXUS gondola external envelope is 1.16 m x 1.16 m x 0.84 m with the total lifted mass (gondola + all experiments + recovery hardware) of approximately 300 kg; the individual experiment allocation is 30 to 112 kg. For this design assume a 60 kg experiment allocation. The mounting frame is a bolted and welded aluminum 6082-T6 space frame built from 30 x 30 x 2 mm square tubing (E 70 GPa, yield 250 MPa, ultimate 290 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3). Frame envelope: 800 x 800 x 500 mm (fitting inside the gondola interior with 50 mm margins). The frame carries a 60 kg experiment at its CoM 300 mm above the gondola mounting floor, mounted via four M8 A4-70 bolts to the gondola's standard T-slot deck. The gondola rises to 25-33 km altitude over ~2 hr, cruises for ~2-5 hr collecting data, then descends on parachute. Mission environment: stratospheric ambient pressure 1 kPa at float altitude, ambient temperature -80 C during ascent through tropopause, stabilizing to -50 C at float after solar-heated gondola interior reaches -20 C average; post-cutdown descent parachute shock and ground impact at 8 m/s on rough terrain. Load cases: (LC1) parachute opening shock: 5 g vertical on 60 kg experiment at frame CoM (equivalent static 2943 N distributed at the CoM); (LC2) ground-impact landing: 10 g on 60 kg (equivalent 5886 N distributed at CoM) with simultaneous 5 g horizontal skid; (LC3) handling / integration: 2 g vertical + 2 g lateral when the gondola is lifted by crane during integration; (LC4) thermal gradient at float: gondola-interior 10 C (solar warm from above) while experiment heat generation brings part of frame to 30 C at hot bosses - thermal delta 20 C; (LC5) cold-soak at -80 C during ascent: frame at -80 C with no mechanical load, but check material stiffness / CTE mismatch with M8 stainless fasteners (alu CTE 23.6e-6 /K, stainless 17.3e-6 /K); (LC6) wind gust during launch pre-release: 50 N horizontal applied at the top of the frame. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 and LC2 mechanical, max von Mises stress must remain below 166 MPa (250 / 1.5 yield FoS per BEXUS user-manual structural recommendation); (R2) frame dry mass (frame only, excluding experiment and fasteners) must not exceed 8 kg; (R3) first natural frequency of the loaded frame (with 60 kg experiment mass at CoM) must exceed 30 Hz to avoid coupling with gondola suspension rocking (typical 0.5 - 5 Hz); (R4) under LC4 thermal and LC5 cold-soak combined, bolt preload loss must remain below 15% (to keep experiment secured); (R5) under LC2 ground impact, CoM vertical deflection at the experiment attachment plate must remain below 3 mm. Fluid, radiation, and buckling analyses not primary gates. Deliverables: STEP AP242 frame, bolt table, mechanical FEA for LC1-LC3 + LC6, thermo-structural for LC4 and LC5, modal report, mass budget.
short_prompt:
Design the primary experiment mounting frame inside an ESA BEXUS stratospheric balloon gondola (gondola 1.16 x 1.16 x 0.84 m, lifted mass ~300 kg, experiment allocation 30-112 kg; take 60 kg here). Gondola rises to 25-33 km over ~2 hr, cruises 2-5 hr, then descends on parachute.

Envelope: welded 6082-T6 aluminum space frame, 30 x 30 x 2 mm square tube, inside 800 x 800 x 500 mm; 60 kg experiment at 300 mm above the gondola deck; 4 x M8 A4-70 bolts to the standard T-slot deck. Float at 1 kPa, -80 C ascent, -50 C float.

Factor regime: BEXUS user-manual yield FoS 1.5 (allowable 166 MPa); frame dry mass <= 8 kg; loaded first frequency > 30 Hz (suspension-rock decouple); thermal + cold-soak bolt preload loss < 15%; CoM deflection at ground impact < 3 mm.

Load cases.
- Parachute-opening shock: 5 g vertical on 60 kg at the CoM (2943 N distributed).
- Ground-impact landing: 10 g vertical + 5 g horizontal skid on 60 kg (5886 N).
- Handling / crane lift: 2 g vertical + 2 g lateral during integration.
- Float thermal gradient: 10 C gondola interior to 30 C hot bosses (20 C delta).
- Ascent cold-soak: -80 C uniform (CTE mismatch with stainless bolts, Al 23.6e-6 vs SST 17.3e-6).
- Pre-release wind gust: 50 N horizontal at the top of the frame.

prompt.title: ESA BEXUS 60 kg Experiment Mounting Frame
prompt.brief: 6082-T6 space frame 800x800x500 mm for 60 kg experiment inside BEXUS gondola, 25-33 km altitude, parachute landing.

load_cases.name: float_thermal_delta
load_cases.application: hot boss 30 C vs interior 10 C

load_cases.name: cold_soak_-80C
load_cases.application: full frame cold soak, CTE mismatch with stainless bolts

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: 250 / 1.5 yield FoS per BEXUS user manual

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam/shell for tubing; TET10 at bolted nodes

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Ascent/descent aero reduced to parachute + gust loads.
exclusions_explained.radiation_analysis: Solar heating captured via gondola interior boundary temperature.
